ss.s: Click on the phone icon when a call comes in.
After identifying the reason for the call, click the Customer Profile button.
In the Interaction Notes, enter “Customer reports slow internet since morning.”
Press Ctrl+N to Save your initial notes.
In the Interaction Notes, type “Intermittent connection; slow speeds reported.”
And press Ctrl+N to save the updated Notes.
Click Create Case.
From the pop-up window, select Technical Issue.
Then click Submit.
Select Connectivity as the issue type.
And click Confirm.
Confirm the customer’s information. If all details are correct, click Verify.
Close the pop-up and continue documenting the interaction.
Type “Verification complete; proceed to troubleshooting.”
Then press Ctrl+N.
Press Ctrl+R or click Run Diagnostics.
Running diagnostics on the customer’s modem checks the connection’s health and identifies potential issues.
After running diagnostics, the system indicates that Signal instability has been detected.
Proceed to click View Details.
Explain to the customer that the diagnostics results confirm the modem is experiencing unstable signal, which may affect connection quality.
Since the customer may choose either option, select one path first, then return and complete the other. When done, click Continue.
Click the Close (X) button on the pop-up window or press Ctrl+X.
Then, click the Refresh Modem button or press Ctrl + F.
Wait for the system to update the status.
Once the process is complete, a confirmation message will appear: “Refresh completed successfully.”
Click the Close (X) button on the pop-up window or press Ctrl+X.
Enter “Modem refresh performed; awaiting reconnection or callback.” in the Interaction Notes.
And press Ctrl+N to save the updated Notes.
Click the Close (X) button on the pop-up window or press Ctrl+X.
Press Ctrl + F or click the Refresh Modem button to refresh the modem.
Give it a moment while the system updates the status.
Once done, you’ll see a message that says, “Refresh completed successfully.”
Click the Close (X) button on the pop-up window or press Ctrl+X.
Then type: “Modem refresh performed; connection stable.”
And press Ctrl+N to save the updated Notes.
Outage Check: Click the Check Outage button or press Ctrl+O to run the outage check.
This will perform an outage check using the customer’s location. Review the system result.
Click the Close (X) button on the pop-up window or press Ctrl+X.
Enter “No outage detected.” in the Interaction Notes.
Line Quality Recheck: Click the Run Diagnostics button or press Ctrl+R to run the diagnostics again.
Observe the updated diagnostic results.
Click the Close (X) button on the pop-up window or press Ctrl+X.
Then complete your notes by typing "Persistent minor signal issues.".
And press Ctrl+N to save the updated Notes.
Click Schedule Technician to begin setting the appointment.
Choose the preferred date and time slot according to the customer’s availability and the system’s scheduling options.
Select June 5th on the calendar.
Available time slots will automatically appear below.
Select the 3:00 PM time slot.
Then click Confirm Appointment to finalize the scheduling.
A pop-up will appear giving you the option to send an email confirmation to the customer's registered email address. Click Yes.
Review the summary email and make any necessary edits. Once finished, click Send.
Click OK to close the Appointment window.
For proper documentation and tracking, enter “Technician scheduled; customer advised. #0123789" in the Notes field.
Press Ctrl+N to save your Notes.
Update the notes with the key actions taken during the call.
Enter “Recapped, summary email sent.”
Click the End call button.
Press Ctrl+S to save the case.
Then click OK and be ready for the next call.
A customer reports slow internet since morning and intermittent disconnections. After greeting the customer and identifying the issue, what should you do next?
